Google just dropped an update that is actually insane. Like not kind of cool insane actually genuinely stop what you're doing insane. They just put a full AI brain inside Google Slides. And it doesn't just help you. It literally builds your presentations for you from a prompt, from rough notes, from a sketch. 30 seconds, done. No design skills needed. No formatting headaches. No wasting 3 hours on a deck that looks average. Anyway, this is the update that makes presentation software feel completely outdated overnight. and I'm going to break down exactly what it does and how you can use it right now. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. And Julian reads every single comment. So, drop something below and he will see it. Okay, let's talk about what Google just did. Why? Because this is one of those updates that actually matters. So, Google just deeply integrated Gemini AI into Google Workspace. And the big one, the one everyone needs to know about is what they did inside Google Slides. This is not a small feature update. This is not a new font pack or a slightly better export button. This is AI being built directly into the tool. Not a separate app, not a chatbot you switch to right there inside slides, ready to build presentations for you from scratch. And the reason this is such a big deal is because of how presentations used to work. You had to do everything. You picked the layout, you formatted the text, you built the charts, you made sure the colors matched, you spent hours making something that looked okay. And honestly, most people are not great at design. So, even after all that time, the slides looked pretty average. That whole process is now gone. AI handles it. Let me break down the three core things this AI update can actually do because these are the features that change your workflow completely. The first one is turning notes into slides. You paste in raw messy notes and AI converts them into a clean formatted structured slide deck automatically. It picks the layout, formats the headings, applies a visual hierarchy and makes everything look professional. You don't touch a single design setting. Think about how useful that is in real life. Let's say you just ran a strategy session for the AI profit boardroom and you've got a page of messy bullet points from the core. Instead of spending an hour turning those into a training deck for your members, you paste them in, hit go, and AI builds the whole thing. Clean slides, proper structure, ready to present. That's the kind of timesaving that actually compounds over a week, a month, a year. The second feature is sketch to chart. You literally draw a rough diagram, something handdrawn and messy, and AI converts it into a clean, editable, professional chart. So if you're mapping out a workflow, a funnel, a process, you can sketch it out fast and AI turns it into something polished and presentation ready. That is not something that existed in mainstream tools before this update. The third feature is automatic style matching. AI reads your existing deck, picks up the fonts, colors, layout style, and visual hierarchy, and then applies that same style to every new slide you generate. Right? So, if you're building out new content and you want everything to stay on brand and consistent, AI handles it automatically. No more going through slide by slide fixing formatting. It just matches. These three features together completely change how long it takes to go from an idea to a finished presentation. Here's the thing about this update that I think most people are going to miss at first. It is not just about saving time on slides. That's the obvious benefit. But the bigger shift is what this represents for productivity software as a whole. Google is not just adding AI as a feature. They are rebuilding workspace around AI. Gemini is going into Docs, into Sheets, into Drive Search. The whole suite is being redesigned so that AI is the brain running underneath everything you do. That means pretty soon you will be able to generate a full business report with a single prompt. Build a spreadsheet from raw data automatically. Search your entire drive using plain English and get exactly what you need instantly. Design a complete presentation from a rough idea in under a minute. And the people who understand how to use these tools early, who build their workflows around AI now, are going to have a serious edge because everyone else is still doing things the old way, still formatting manually, still clicking around, still spending time on stuff that AI can handle in seconds. Links are in the description. Go check it out and then come back because we've still got the practical stuff to cover. Let me give you three workflows you can start using today. The first workflow is the fast deck. You go into Google Slides, open Gemini inside the app, and you type a prompt something like create a five slide overview of how AI automation helps online businesses work smarter and deliver better results for their clients. AI builds the deck. You clean it up in a couple of minutes. you have a polished presentation ready to go in under 5 minutes total. That workflow used to take hours. The second workflow is the notes to training content workflow. If you run a community, do coaching calls, lead workshops, or create any kind of educational content, this one is going to save you a huge amount of time. You take your raw notes from a session, paste them into Gemini inside slides, and tell it to turn those notes into a clean, structured slide deck for an online training session. It formats everything, builds the structure, and creates something your audience can actually follow. You go from raw notes to finished training material fast. The third workflow is the brand consistency workflow. You upload an existing deck that already matches your brand. You tell AI to match the style. Then every new slide you generate automatically looks consistent. Same fonts, same colors, same layout logic. If you create a lot of content or manage a team that puts together presentations regularly, this one alone is worth paying attention to because inconsistent branding on slides is one of those things that makes everything look less professional and AI just solves it automatically. I want to zoom out for a second because there is a line that I think nails what is actually happening here. We are moving from presentation software to presentation generation. That is the shift. Slides are no longer designed manually. They are generated by AI. You provide the raw material, the idea, the notes, the sketch and AI handles the creation. And this is not just Google. This is the direction every major productivity tool is moving. Microsoft is doing it with copilot inside PowerPoint and Word. Notion has AI built in. The tools that do not have AI builtin are starting to feel outdated. But Google has a massive advantage here because Workspace is already the default for so many businesses, schools, and teams around the world. By putting Gemini directly inside those tools, they are not asking people to change their workflow. They are upgrading the workflow people already have. That is smart and it means adoption is going to be fast. So, let's bring this together. Google just made presentations automatic AI builds slides from prompts, converts rough sketches into clean charts, matches your brand style across every new slide, and turns messy notes into polished structured decks. It is available now inside Google Workspace. If you are not using this yet, start today. Pick one of the three workflows I gave you. Try it on something real and see how much time it saves you.
